title: Encrypt etc

input_row.title: Encrypt etc
input_row.description: ETCD is not enabled by default; enabling it can provide an additional layer of protection.
input_row.rationale: You can enable etcd encryption for your cluster to provide an additional layer of data security. For example, it can help protect the loss of sensitive data if an etcd backup is exposed to the incorrect parties.
input_row.audit: Execute oc get openshiftapiserver -o=jsonpath='{range .items[0].status.conditions[?(@.type=="Encrypted")]}{.reason}{"\n"}{.message} {"\n"}' The output should show EncryptionCompleted upon successful encryption: EncryptionCompleted All resources encrypted: routes.route.openshift.io, oauthaccesstokens.oauth.openshift.io, oauthauthorizetokens.oauth.openshift.io
input_row.remediation: 1. Modify the API Server Object oc edit apiserver 2. Set the encryption field type to aescbc: spec: encryption: type: aescbc 3. Save the file to apply the changes. The encryption process starts. It can take 20 minutes or longer for this process to complete, depending on the size of your cluster.
input_row.impact: With encrpytion on etcd the following contents are encrypted at rest Secrets Config maps Routes OAuth access tokens OAuth authorize tokens
input_row.default_value: By default ETCD is not encrypted

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit can leverage the provided CLI command to check the encryption status of the etcd.
- The IBM Cloud CLI can execute commands to retrieve this information, confirming that it can be automated.
- The control is standard across cloud services for encryption settings, indicating that it can probably be managed through APIs.
- The remediation suggests changing configurations through the CLI, signaling an objective way to validate compliance.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_openshift_etcd_encryption_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. Use the command `oc get openshiftapiserver -o=jsonpath='{range .items[0].status.conditions[?(@.type=="Encrypted")]}{.reason}{\"\\n\"}{.message} {\"\\n\"}'` to check if etcd encryption is enabled.
2. Validate that the output shows `EncryptionCompleted` to confirm that encryption is successfully applied.
3. If the output does not include `EncryptionCompleted`, the check should return a Fail state.
4. Specifically, check that the message "All resources encrypted" contains references to `routes.route.openshift.io`, `oauthaccesstokens.oauth.openshift.io`, and `oauthauthorizetokens.oauth.openshift.io`, ensuring all necessary resources are encrypted.
5. The compliance criteria is to verify that the status of etcd indicates it is encrypted and all relevant resources are secured.

This automation will ensure that the etcd encryption status within the IBM Cloud environment is continually monitored and compliant with the CIS guidelines efficiently.
